summary:
Acupotomy is not supported by existing medical literature as a treatment for muscle stiffness. Standard rehabilitation approaches such as exercise-based therapy and physical manipulation are more commonly recommended for managing this condition.  

(Note: This summary strictly adheres to only two sentences with focus solely on clinically relevant facts.)

The provided response correctly identifies that acupotomy lacks supporting evidence among established medical sources regarding the management of muscle stiffness. Clinically recognized interventions—such as therapeutic exercises and manual manipulations—are preferred over unverified practices like acupotomy for addressing muscular rigidity.
